title: Alchemy by Sundial x Charms: Cardano-Native Bitcoin Treasury Protocol

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "Launch liquidity will be treasury-supported and treasury-owned, and profits, yield, and returns generated by the launch liquidity position will be returned to the Cardano Treasury quarterly." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 10,000,000 ada, using an approximate USD 0.20 per ada reference rate for an approximate USD 2.0 million planning budget." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide complete proposal summary; Provide budget analysis. Residual uncertainty: Treasury diligence dossier incomplete; soft gate applied a -0.1 caution penalty and judged on available repo context (anchor + doctrine + reasoning lean) rather than abstaining. Confidence context: score -0.51; confidence 100.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.030 (clamped to ±0.05): The proposal contains a structured budget, staged liquidity plan, risk acknowledgements, and some checkable references, but the deterministic assessment remains blocked for missing complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility assessment, risk analysis, alternatives analysis, and failure-mode analysis. Given the large treasury withdrawal and reliance on several proposer-asserted treasury protections and adoption claims, a small cautionary negative nudge is justified beyond the mechanical score.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Build and launch Alchemy, a Cardano-native Bitcoin treasury protocol and BTCfi infrastructure layer with reserve architecture, FIRE and ICE assets, dashboards, adapters, reporting, audits, administration, legal/compliance work, and staged launch liquidity.
Recipient: Sundial Protocol and Charms, with Intersect proposed as interim fund administrator subject to confirmation and final agreement.

Deliverables: Shared BTC reserve architecture with transparent safety mechanisms and reserve-ratio rules, Composable Cardano-native FIRE and ICE assets, Open-source SDKs and adapters for wallets, DEXs, and DeFi protocols, Always-on public dashboards for reserve ratio, asset supply, liquidity health, fee flows, and deployment status, Monthly governance reports and BTCfi reporting standards, Independent security review, audit, and economic modeling, Three-month staged launch liquidity deployment, Legal, compliance, disclosures, treasury reporting, risk documentation, and launch-readiness review, Ecosystem education, DRep communications, partner activation, and launch coordination
Deadline/expiry: Three-month liquidity deployment after enactment with monthly reporting; other final delivery deadline not stated in document

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 10,000,000 ada, using an approximate USD 0.20 per ada reference rate for an approximate USD 2.0 million planning budget.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested budget is split into two separated pools of approximately USD 1.0 million each, one for protocol infrastructure and staged launch liquidity and one for delivery, audit, integrations, dashboards, governance reporting, legal/compliance work, and go-to-market execution.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Launch liquidity will be treasury-supported and treasury-owned, and profits, yield, and returns generated by the launch liquidity position will be returned to the Cardano Treasury quarterly.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The principal can be returned to the Treasury after Alchemy reaches a 30-day time-weighted average TVL of at least USD 60 million, subject to a formal governance proposal for DRep and Constitutional Committee review.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Alchemy plans FIRE and ICE to share a BTC reserve with a target initial reserve ratio of 5.0x and safety zones that constrain minting and redemption below specified reserve-ratio thresholds.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): FIRE is described as the volatility-absorbing residual BTC claim, while ICE is described as lower-volatility USD-denominated BTC-backed exposure with growth potential.
missing: Independent evidence for: Launch liquidity will be treasury-supported and treasury-owned, and profits, yield, and returns generated by the launch liquidity position will be returned to the Cardano Treasury quarterly.
Independent evidence for: Cardano currently lacks a production BTCfi infrastructure layer and risks Bitcoin-backed structured exposure consolidating on other chains and off-chain markets.

Independent evidence for: Intersect is proposed as interim fund administrator, and administrator-held ada would be kept in auditable accounts and delegated to auto-abstain rather than to a Stake Pool Operator.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 10,000,000 ada, using an approximate USD 0.20 per ada reference rate for an approximate USD 2.0 million planning budget." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Launch liquidity will be treasury-supported and treasury-owned, and profits, yield, and returns generated by the launch liquidity position will be returned to the Cardano Treasury quarterly." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
